      • [ - ] PunterBloke - member since 01-Dec-24

        Arsenal had always set-pieces to count on,nowadays even own goals are helping their way. But when it the trend ends one day, I don't know how they gonna manage to score goals from open play. The competition is becoming challenging and is toughening, getting stiff and unpredictable. Now I see Man City, Aston Villa and Arsenal as contenders; Chelsea,we can forget about it until Maresca fixes his rotation issue.

      • [ - ] haunani - member since 21-Nov-25

        Arsenal usually control the ball and move it quickly through midfield, working the wide areas and trying to create openings with combinations. Brighton tend to stay organised and compact, winning the ball back and then pushing forward with direct passes when they can. It feels like Arsenal focus on possession and building play, while Brighton look to make the most of their forward moments.       • [ - ] GoonerRSA - member since 07-Oct-22

        Well Emirates is a fortress this season and only Man City have survived there and they used some never-seen tactics to avoid a repeat of what happened last season. I expect not only Brighton but Aston Villa as well to lose there. If Arsenal don't win the league it will simple because of the points they drop away... sometimes unnecessarily. 2-1
